title: `--expose=8000/sctp` causes Error: protocol SCTP is not allowed
body:
### Issue Description

Unable to run container image built with `EXPOSE 8000/sctp` in the `Dockerfile` or equivalently run with `--expose=8000/sctp` fail to run as `root` or a non-root user with the following error:
> Error: validating protocols for exposed port 8000: protocol SCTP is not allowed for exposed ports



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman run -it --expose 8000/sctp ubuntu:22.04 bash`

or
 
2. `podman run -it oaisoftwarealliance/oai-amf:v2.1.0 bash` (This image is built with `EXPOSE 38412/sctp` )


### Describe the results you received

> Error: validating protocols for exposed port 8000: protocol SCTP is not allowed for exposed ports

### Describe the results you expected

I expect it to run regardless, as privileged or rootless, as `--expose` or `EXPOSE` should not have any effect on the runtime. 

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/c12c86e303f502684a84f817ad0004582ab67a1f/docs/source/markdown/options/expose.md?plain=1#L7-L12



### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Bare-metal Ubuntu 22.04 Server

### Additional information

`podman run -it --publish 8000:8000/sctp ubuntu:22.04 bash `  behaves as described in 

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/c12c86e303f502684a84f817ad0004582ab67a1f/docs/source/markdown/options/publish.md?plain=1#L15-L18

It runs successfully when run as `root` and when run as a non-root user I get the following error:
> Error: rootlessport spec was not validated?


## Solution (possibly)

The following fix worked for me.

```diff
--- a/pkg/specgen/generate/ports.go
+++ b/pkg/specgen/generate/ports.go
@@ -356,7 +356,7 @@ func createPortMappings(s *specgen.SpecGenerator, imageData *libimage.ImageData)
                        if port == 0 {
                                return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot expose 0 as it is not a valid port number")
                        }
-                       protocols, err := checkProtocol(proto, false)
+                       protocols, err := checkProtocol(proto, true)
                        if err != nil {
                                return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("validating protocols for exposed port %d: %w", port, err)
                        }

```

comments.body:
This is an issue in the documentation, because available rootless network back-ends don't support SCTP.

For pasta, this is the related [tracker](https://bugs.passt.top/show_bug.cgi?id=3), but it's such a low priority that we never got to it.

Same for slirp4netns: there's a [TODO](https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns/blob/ee1542e1532e6a7f266b8b6118973ab3b10a8bb5/api.c#L112) in the port forwarding implementation.

@sbrivio-rh No not really, --expose != --publish as mentioned in the linked docs in thus nothing but adding the port to the metadata which will be used on podman commit to add a sctp port to the image config.
But given expose is never used by default and users have to explicitly publish the port it doesn't really make sense to error here as it just prevent users from being able to run the image which a sctp port in them defined.

@sreevatsank1999 I think your diff make sense, although if all callers pass true then we should just remove the arg allowSCTP arg from that function.

Looking at the git log to try to figure out why expose is special and not allowed for expose I cannot find any reason, it was added in https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/7ac3d906b5c080803f7898d3307af87bb57ad436 but without any reason given. @mheon Can we remove that sctp check? I cannot see why this would cause any harm.

I have no memory of why I did that. My two questions would be: Can we commit SCTP exposed ports to an image, and if we make a container from an image with an SCTP exposed port and use `-P`, does it properly publish the port?

If both of those are true I'm fine with removing the check.

> with an SCTP exposed port and use -P, does it properly publish the port?

As root it should work, there we have have support for sctp forwarding. For rootless neither pasta, slirp4netns or rootlessport support sctp today so these will error at a later stage on startup.
